race.name: GFNY Aguascalientes
race.tagline: BE A PRO FOR A DAY in Mexico's cycling hotbed with views of Cerro del Muerto

climate.description: Warm, welcoming highland climate with thermal baths; named for 'hot waters'[1][3]

terrain.surface: Rolling hills with max gradient 6.3%, quiet roads, cactus trees, desert landscapes, and constant views of Cerro del Muerto mountain range[1]
features: 3%, quiet roads, cactus trees, desert landscapes, and constant views of Cerro del Muerto mountain range[1]

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 71. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 66.

course_description.character: Starts/finishes Isla San Marcos. Rolling hills w/ 6.3% max gradient, 4 notable climbs. **Suffering Zone 1: El Ocote** - 5km early climb w/ rock climbing range vistas. Quiet roads + Cerro del Muerto views after[1]

logistics.transport: Aguascalientes Airport (AGU) 20min from hotels. Direct flights: LAX(4h), HOU(2.5-3h), MIA(4h), BOG(5-6h). National: TIJ(3h), CUN(2h). Drive: Mexico City(4-5h), Guadalajara(2-3h)[1][3]

history.origin_story: Inaugural event March 22, 2026. Announced Dec 2025 as GFNY HQ team 'homecoming' in Mexico's cycling hotbed[1][2]

biased_opinion.summary: GFNY's Mexico expansion hits with a culturally rich, scenic highland challenge. El Ocote climb and thermal bath recovery make this inaugural event instantly bucket-list worthy for LatAm endurance cyclists.

final_verdict.one_liner: BE A PRO FOR A DAY in Mexico's cycling hotbed with views of Cerro del Muerto
